question: Based on the stacked area chart of quarterly restaurant performance indicators, which quarter appears to have the best overall performance?

options.A: Q1 appears best because it has the lowest customer complaint area.
options.B: The chart cannot determine overall performance because it combines unrelated metrics with different units.
options.C: Q1 appears best because it has the highest combined stacked area across all indicators.
options.D: Q3 appears best because its indicators are the most balanced.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a cumulative area. These metrics have different units and meanings, so adding them together creates a meaningless total. The stacked area cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric analysis is valid.
